title: poa nemoralis proles glaucantha
intro: Poa nemoralis proles glaucantha, also known as rough meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the Poa nemoralis grass species. This perennial grass is native to Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of Poa nemoralis proles glaucantha can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
